title: Backscroll (bsc) — v0
overview: Backscroll is a local-only time machine for the terminal: it records every shell command (text, output, cwd, exit code, timestamp, duration, git branch) into a searchable SQLite+FTS5 store so you can later find past commands — including their OUTPUT, which is what makes 'the one that worked' answerable. v0 ships scaffold+CI, storage layer, PTY recording (bsc rec) plus a zsh hook (bsc init zsh), filtered search, bsc show, an interactive fuzzy picker that copies to clipboard, and first-class privacy (redaction, exclude config, pause). 100% local, no network.
scopeChallenge: Greenfield repo (only .claude/ exists), Node v22.12, fzf NOT installed. Phase 0 locked four decisions with the user: (1) capture = PTY-wrapped subshell that auto-injects a shell integration emitting OSC 133 markers carrying metadata, with heuristic prompt detection only as fallback — chosen over hooks-only (drops the headline output feature) and pure heuristics (fragile across prompt themes); (2) EXPANSION mode (nothing to reuse); (3) custom Node TUI picker (fzf absent, zero external dep); (4) zsh-only for v0 (bash/fish deferred). Explicit non-goals to avoid over-building: no daemon, sync, web UI, encryption-at-rest, stats, replay/asciinema export, bash/fish, or config UI.

existingCodeLeverage.subProblem: Storage, capture, CLI, UI — all application code

failureModes.risk: OSC 133 markers split across PTY read chunks cause dropped or garbled segmentation
failureModes.mitigation: Buffer partial escape sequences in the parser across feed() calls; explicit boundary test in TASK-030.

failureModes.risk: Prompt themes (powerlevel10k, transient/right prompts) defeat the heuristic fallback
failureModes.mitigation: Markers are the primary, deterministic path; heuristic is only a non-fatal safety net and is allowed to be degraded.

failureModes.risk: Native deps (node-pty, better-sqlite3) fail to build on macOS/Linux/CI
failureModes.mitigation: Pin known-good versions; CI matrix builds them on both OSes so breakage is loud, not silent.

failureModes.risk: Secrets leak into storage before redaction runs
failureModes.mitigation: Redaction runs in the persist pipeline before insertCommand, default-on; unit-tested against captured insert args (TASK-033); DB dir is 0700 and gitignored.

failureModes.risk: Unbounded output (yes, cat bigfile, tail -f) OOMs the recorder or bloats the DB
failureModes.mitigation: Per-command maxOutputBytes cap (default 1 MB) truncates with a marker and stops buffering (explicit AC in TASK-012).

failureModes.risk: Synchronous SQLite write + redaction regex on the PTY thread janks the interactive shell
failureModes.mitigation: better-sqlite3 is synchronous; the output cap bounds redaction/write cost, persist runs only at commandEnd (not per chunk), and WAL keeps writes fast. Full async write queue deferred to v1.

failureModes.risk: PTY teardown leaves the user's terminal stuck in raw mode
failureModes.mitigation: Always restore termios in finally/exit handlers, including SIGINT and error paths.

failureModes.risk: FTS index drifts out of sync with commands/output rows
failureModes.mitigation: Single transaction writes commands+output+search_fts together; store-managed rather than multi-table triggers; round-trip test in TASK-031.

failureModes.risk: search.ts edited by two tasks causes a conflict
failureModes.mitigation: TASK-025 depends on TASK-022 so the shared file is written sequentially.
Negative test cases for benign text; conservative key-name matching; visible «redacted:KIND» markers.

testCoverageMap.codepath: Secret masking on the write path
OSC 133 marker parsing including split chunks
Stream to per-command segmentation plus heuristic fallback

tasks.title: Project scaffold & TypeScript config
tasks.description: Create package.json with bin { bsc: dist/cli.js }, deps (node-pty, better-sqlite3, commander, picocolors), devDeps (typescript, jest, ts-jest, @types/jest, @types/node, @types/better-sqlite3, eslint, typescript-eslint, prettier), and scripts (build, dev, test, lint, format). Add tsconfig.json (strict, NodeNext, outDir dist) and .gitignore (node_modules, dist, *.sqlite*).
acceptanceCriteria: npm install succeeds and npx tsc --noEmit runs with zero config errors on an empty src/
tsconfig.json has strict:true and emits to dist/
.gitignore excludes node_modules/, dist/, and *.sqlite* so the DB is never committed

tasks.title: Lint / format / test tooling configs
tasks.description: Create eslint.config.js (flat config, typescript-eslint recommended), .prettierrc.json, and jest.config.js (ts-jest preset, testEnvironment node, testMatch test/**/*.test.ts). Do not modify package.json.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run lint runs eslint over src/ with no config errors
npm test invokes jest and reports no-tests cleanly (exit 0 with --passWithNoTests)
Prettier and eslint do not conflict on a sample formatted file

tasks.title: Domain types & contracts
tasks.description: Create src/types.ts exporting SessionRecord, CommandRecord (source: 'pty'|'hook'), OutputBlock, SearchFilters (cwd?, successOnly?, since?, until?, limit?, query), SearchResult (command + snippet + rank), BackscrollConfig (excludeCommands, excludeDirs, redactionExtraPatterns, redactionEnabled, maxOutputBytes), RedactionMatch.
acceptanceCriteria: All types compile under strict mode with no any in public shapes
CommandRecord.exitCode is number|null and source is the literal union
SearchFilters makes every filter optional except query

tasks.title: SQLite schema (sessions · commands · output · FTS5)
tasks.description: Create src/db/schema.sql: sessions; commands (+ indices on cwd, exit_code, started_at); output (1:1 with commands, ON DELETE CASCADE); contentless search_fts(command, output) with tokenize='porter unicode61'. WAL + foreign_keys pragmas applied by TASK-007.
acceptanceCriteria: Loading the schema into a fresh :memory: better-sqlite3 db succeeds with no SQL errors
commands.session_id and output.command_id both cascade on delete
An FTS MATCH query against search_fts returns rows after a manual insert

tasks.title: Data-dir & path resolution
tasks.description: Create src/paths.ts: resolve data dir ($XDG_DATA_HOME/backscroll or ~/.local/share/backscroll, with $BACKSCROLL_DIR override); expose dbPath(), configPath(), pauseMarkerPath(), ensureDataDir(). Create dir with 0700 perms.
acceptanceCriteria: ensureDataDir() creates the dir recursively and is idempotent on a second call
$BACKSCROLL_DIR overrides the default location
Created directory has owner-only (0700) permissions

tasks.title: Config loader
tasks.description: Create src/config.ts: load JSON config from configPath(), deep-merge over defaults (redactionEnabled:true, empty exclude lists, empty extra patterns, maxOutputBytes:1000000), return typed BackscrollConfig. Tolerate missing/malformed file (warn, never throw).
acceptanceCriteria: Missing config file returns defaults without throwing
Malformed JSON logs a warning and returns defaults (does not crash recording)
User values override defaults; unspecified keys keep defaults

tasks.title: Database open & migrate
tasks.description: Create src/db/database.ts: open better-sqlite3 at dbPath(), apply schema.sql idempotently (guarded by IF NOT EXISTS), set WAL + foreign_keys pragmas, expose getDb() singleton and closeDb().
acceptanceCriteria: First open creates all tables; second open is a no-op with no errors
PRAGMA foreign_keys reports 1 and journal_mode reports wal after open
Opening against a read-only directory surfaces a clear error

tasks.title: Store API
tasks.description: Create src/db/store.ts: createSession(), endSession(id), insertCommand(record) writing commands+output+search_fts in one transaction, search(filters):SearchResult[] (FTS MATCH + cwd/exit/time filters + limit, ordered by rank), getCommandById(id), getRecentCommands(limit). Prepared statements.
acceptanceCriteria: insertCommand then search finds the row by a term in its output
search with successOnly excludes non-zero exits; cwd is a prefix match; limit caps results
A command with no output (hook source) leaves output row absent but stays searchable by command text

tasks.title: Redaction engine (privacy — pre-storage)
tasks.description: Create src/redaction/redact.ts: redact(text, config):string implementing layered patterns (secret-key assignments KEY=value, known token shapes AWS/GitHub/Slack/JWT/PEM, generic long hex/base64, plus config extra patterns). Export rule list for testing. Replace with «redacted:KIND».
acceptanceCriteria: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=..., a ghp_ token, and a Bearer eyJ... JWT are all masked
Benign strings like PATH=/usr/bin and ordinary prose are left untouched
Config extra patterns apply; redactionEnabled:false returns text verbatim

tasks.title: ANSI utilities
tasks.description: Create src/capture/ansi.ts: stripAnsi(s) removing CSI/SGR/OSC and other escape sequences, returning clean text for storage and search.
acceptanceCriteria: Colored output (\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m) reduces to red
Cursor-movement and clear-line sequences are removed without eating surrounding text
Input with no escapes is returned unchanged

tasks.title: OSC 133 marker parser
tasks.description: Create src/capture/osc133.ts: state machine scanning a stream for ESC ] 133 ; {A|B|C|D} [;params] ST/BEL emitting typed events (promptStart, commandStart{cmd,cwd,branch}, commandEnd{exitCode,durationMs}). Decode base64 params. Tolerate markers split across chunk boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: A full 133;C;cmd=<b64>... then 133;D;0;dur=12 sequence emits commandStart + commandEnd with decoded fields
A marker split across two feed() calls is still parsed correctly
Non-133 OSC sequences and malformed params are ignored without throwing

tasks.title: Output segmenter (+ heuristic fallback)
tasks.description: Create src/capture/segmenter.ts: consume PTY stream via the OSC 133 parser, accumulate bytes between commandStart and commandEnd as a command's output, emit OutputBlocks. Enforce a configurable output cap (maxOutputBytes): once a command's output exceeds the cap, stop buffering and append a …[truncated N bytes] marker to bound memory against runaway commands (yes, cat bigfile, tail -f). Fallback: detect prompt redraws when no markers. Strip ANSI from stored output.
acceptanceCriteria: A scripted two-command marker stream yields exactly two OutputBlocks with correct command/exit/output
Output between markers is captured then ANSI-stripped and assigned to the right command
Output exceeding maxOutputBytes is truncated with a …[truncated N bytes] marker and buffering stops (no unbounded memory growth)
A marker-less stream still produces >=1 block via heuristic fallback rather than crashing or losing everything

tasks.title: zsh integration snippet (generator + template)
tasks.description: Create src/shell/zsh-integration.zsh (preexec/precmd emitting OSC 133 A/C/D with base64 cmd/cwd/branch payloads) and src/shell/integration.ts exporting zshSnippet({forRec}) returning it as a string. forRec variant is auto-sourced inside bsc rec; install variant is printed by bsc init zsh.
acceptanceCriteria: Sourcing the snippet in real zsh and running a command emits a 133;C and 133;D pair (verify via cat -v)
The snippet computes git branch without erroring outside a git repo (empty branch, no stderr noise)
integration.ts returns valid zsh for both forRec:true and forRec:false

tasks.title: Recording gate — pause & exclude (privacy)
tasks.description: Create src/capture/recording-gate.ts: isPaused() (presence of pauseMarkerPath()), setPaused(bool), shouldRecord(command, cwd, config) returning false on exclude-pattern match (glob/substring for commands and dirs).
acceptanceCriteria: With the pause marker present, isPaused() is true and nothing is recorded
A command matching excludeCommands and a cwd under excludeDirs are both rejected by shouldRecord
Empty exclude lists allow everything (default behavior)

tasks.title: PTY recorder — terminal mechanics (make-or-break node)
tasks.description: Create src/capture/pty-recorder.ts: spawn $SHELL via node-pty with a temp ZDOTDIR rcfile sourcing the forRec integration; pipe child<->parent stdio transparently; tee output into the segmenter; handle SIGWINCH resize and clean teardown (always restore termios). Hand each OutputBlock to the persist pipeline (TASK-033) — this task owns terminal mechanics, not the storage/privacy decision. Return child exit code.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc rec gives a fully interactive shell; typing/colors/resize behave like a normal terminal
Each command's OutputBlock is forwarded to the persist pipeline with correct command/exit/output
Terminal raw mode is always restored on exit, including on child crash or parent SIGINT (no broken terminal)

tasks.title: Time parsing & humanize utils
tasks.description: Create src/util/time.ts: parseSince(s)/parseUntil(s) accepting relative (3w, 2d, 1mo, 45m) and ISO dates -> epoch ms; humanizeRelative(ms) and humanizeDuration(ms). Inject 'now' for testability.
acceptanceCriteria: 3w resolves to ~21 days before a passed-in reference time (no hidden Date.now())
An ISO string parses to the correct epoch ms
An unparseable token throws a clear error surfaced to the user

tasks.title: Clipboard utility
tasks.description: Create src/ui/clipboard.ts: copyToClipboard(text) using pbcopy (darwin), wl-copy/xclip/xsel (linux); detect availability and fail gracefully with a message if none found. Pass text via stdin, never shell-interpolated.
acceptanceCriteria: On macOS, copied text is retrievable via pbpaste
When no clipboard tool is present, returns a handled failure and tells the user (no unhandled throw)
Text with newlines/quotes is copied without corruption (stdin, not shell interpolation)

tasks.title: Result formatting & match highlighting
tasks.description: Create src/ui/format.ts: render SearchResult[] as aligned rows (id, ✓/✗ glyph, relative time, cwd, command) with picocolors, highlighting matched query terms in command + a one-line output snippet. Respect NO_COLOR and non-TTY.
acceptanceCriteria: Matched substrings are highlighted; exit 0 shows ✓ and non-zero shows ✗
NO_COLOR=1 (or non-TTY) disables color codes entirely
Very long commands/cwd are truncated to terminal width without breaking column alignment

tasks.title: bsc init zsh command
tasks.description: Create src/commands/init.ts: print the install-variant zsh snippet (TASK-013) to stdout with a one-line comment instructing the user to add eval "$(bsc init zsh)" to .zshrc. Error on unsupported shell arg.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc init zsh prints valid sourceable zsh to stdout (nothing else on stdout)
bsc init bash exits non-zero with 'only zsh supported in v0'
Output is pure shell (no color codes) so eval "$(bsc init zsh)" works

tasks.title: bsc rec command
tasks.description: Create src/commands/rec.ts: create a session in the store, invoke the PTY recorder, end the session on exit, print a 'recording -> <db>' banner to stderr at start. Exit with the child shell's exit code. Refuse nested rec.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc rec opens a subshell; exiting returns control and sets ended_at on the session row
The startup banner goes to stderr, not stdout
Nested bsc rec inside an existing rec session is detected and refused with a clear message

tasks.title: bsc pause / resume / status command
tasks.description: Create src/commands/pause.ts: pause creates the pause marker, resume removes it, status prints whether recording is paused plus the data-dir/db location.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc pause then bsc status reports paused; bsc resume then bsc status reports active
resume when not paused is a no-op (no error)
status prints the resolved DB path so users can verify where data lives

tasks.title: bsc search command (non-interactive)
tasks.description: Create src/commands/search.ts: parse <query> + --cwd --success --since --until --limit, build SearchFilters (parse times via TASK-016), call store.search, print via format. Non-interactive path; picker layered in TASK-025.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc search docker --success --limit 5 returns <=5 exit-0 results matching docker
--since 3w filters out older rows; --cwd restricts by directory prefix
Empty result prints a friendly 'no matches' (exit 0); invalid --since errors clearly

tasks.title: bsc show <id> command
tasks.description: Create src/commands/show.ts: fetch command + output by id, print a metadata header (command, cwd, branch, exit, time, duration) then the full stored (redacted) output. Error if id not found.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc show <id> prints the full output block for that command
A non-existent id exits non-zero with 'command #<id> not found'
A hook-sourced command with no output prints the header + '(no output captured)' without crashing

tasks.title: Interactive TUI fuzzy picker
tasks.description: Create src/ui/picker.ts: raw-mode keypress loop over a result set — type to fuzzy-narrow, up/down to move, an output-preview pane for the highlighted row (getCommandById), Enter copies the selected command to clipboard (TASK-017) and exits, esc/Ctrl-C quits. Restore terminal state on exit.
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow keys move selection and the preview pane updates to the highlighted command's output
Enter copies the highlighted command and exits cleanly; esc exits without copying
Terminal raw mode is always restored on exit, including Ctrl-C / error (no broken terminal)

tasks.title: Wire picker into bsc search
tasks.description: Modify src/commands/search.ts: when stdout is a TTY and --no-pick is absent, route results into the picker (TASK-024); otherwise keep the plain list. Add the --no-pick flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Interactive TTY run opens the picker; --no-pick or a piped/non-TTY run prints the plain list
An empty result set does not open an empty picker (prints 'no matches')
The copied command is reported to the user after the picker exits

tasks.title: CLI entry & command registration
tasks.description: Create src/cli.ts: #!/usr/bin/env node commander program named bsc, register init/rec/search/show/pause/resume/status; global try/catch printing clean errors with proper exit codes; --version/--help.
acceptanceCriteria: bsc --help lists all subcommands; bsc --version prints the package version
An unknown subcommand exits non-zero with a helpful message
Thrown errors produce a single clean stderr line + non-zero exit (no raw stack dump by default)

tasks.title: CI workflow
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml: matrix {macos-latest, ubuntu-latest} x node {20,22}; steps checkout, setup-node, npm ci, npm run build, npm run lint, npm test. Cache npm.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow YAML is valid and triggers on push + PR
Matrix covers macOS and Linux on node 20 and 22
Native deps (node-pty, better-sqlite3) build in CI or the job fails loudly

tasks.title: README
tasks.description: Create README.md: pitch, ASCII architecture diagram, quickstart (npm i -g, bsc init zsh, bsc rec, bsc search), prominent Privacy section (local-only, redaction, exclude/pause), and a short 'How this differs from atuin' note — key point: Backscroll records command OUTPUT so 'the one that worked' is answerable.
acceptanceCriteria: Quickstart commands match the actual implemented CLI surface (init/rec/search/show/pause)
Privacy section states 100% local, no network and documents redaction + exclude + pause
The atuin comparison explicitly names output-capture as the differentiator

tasks.title: Redaction tests
tasks.description: Create test/redaction.test.ts: positive masking (AWS/GitHub/JWT/PEM/KEY=value), negative cases (no over-redaction of benign text), config extra patterns + disable toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: All documented secret shapes are masked; benign strings are untouched
An extra config pattern masks a custom token
redactionEnabled:false returns input verbatim

tasks.title: Capture tests (OSC 133 + segmenter)
tasks.description: Create test/osc133.test.ts and test/segmenter.test.ts: feed scripted byte streams (incl. chunk-split markers and a marker-less fallback stream) and assert correct events/blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-command marker stream produces two correct OutputBlocks
A marker split across two feeds parses correctly
A marker-less stream produces output via heuristic fallback (no crash, no total loss)

tasks.title: Store / search / FTS tests
tasks.description: Create test/store.test.ts: against an in-memory db, assert insert->FTS search round-trips and each filter (cwd, successOnly, since/until, limit) behaves correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert then search by an output term returns the row
successOnly excludes non-zero exits; cwd prefix filter and limit work
since/until window excludes out-of-range rows

tasks.title: Recording-gate tests (privacy control)
tasks.description: Create test/recording-gate.test.ts: cover pause/exclude privacy controls — pause marker honored, exclude-command and exclude-dir patterns reject, empty exclude lists allow everything.
acceptanceCriteria: With the pause marker present isPaused() is true; absent, false
A command matching excludeCommands and a cwd under excludeDirs are both rejected by shouldRecord
Empty exclude lists allow all commands (default-permissive)

tasks.title: Persist pipeline (gate -> redact -> insert)
tasks.description: Create src/capture/persist.ts and test/persist.test.ts. persist.ts exports a function taking an OutputBlock + config that (1) checks isPaused() and shouldRecord() (TASK-014), dropping the record if either rejects; (2) runs redact() (TASK-009) on command AND output; (3) calls insertCommand (TASK-008). Isolates the storage/privacy decision from PTY mechanics so the redaction-before-write guarantee is unit-testable without spawning a shell.
acceptanceCriteria: A normal block is redacted (command + output) and inserted exactly once
When paused or when shouldRecord rejects, nothing is inserted (verified against a fake/in-memory store)
Redaction runs before insertCommand so a secret-bearing block never reaches the store unmasked (asserted on captured insert args)
